programs.title: Strangest Things
programs.desc: A stone sphere, a hook and five fragments of wood are the only objects ever discovered inside the Great Pyramid of Giza; experts investigate if they can explain the mysteries behind the only surviving Wonder of the Ancient World.

programs.title: Strange Evidence
programs.desc: Cameras capture stunned pedestrians running for cover as droplets of fire begin to rain down on the streets of St. Petersburg, Russia; experts investigate a network of nearby tram lines and the effect of a sudden lightning storm.

programs.title: Strange Evidence
programs.desc: Cameras capture an enormous fireball erupting along with a series of explosions in a North Korean border town; experts investigate and discover a cramped and overcrowded housing project linked to a deadly propane tank disaster.

programs.title: Strange Evidence
programs.desc: Cameras capture a towering 1,600 foot pillar of flame erupting from the Caspian Sea off the coast of Azerbaijan; experts investigate a potential link to the nation's weapons arsenal and military aspirations over disputed neighboring territory.

programs.title: Strange Evidence
programs.desc: A vacationing family films waves of fire breaking along the shores of an Indonesian island; experts investigate a possible connection to a classified WWII beach-burning weapon developed by the British to incinerate invading armies.

programs.title: Strange Evidence
programs.desc: Motorcyclists film a bizarre, elongated humanoid creature walking along a road in Jharkhand, India; experts investigate the violent history behind an organized cult of professional robbers and murderers who targeted unsuspecting travelers.
